name: Caramels
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 9-inch square cake pan with extra butter or margarine.
2. Melt the 1 cup of butter in a large saucepan over medium heat.
3. Add sugar and salt to the melted butter, mixing well.
4. Add corn syrup and milk, stirring constantly until the mixture boils.
5. Reduce the heat to moderate and continue cooking, stirring constantly, until a candy thermometer registers 245 degrees or a small amount of the mixture forms a firm ball when dropped into cold water.
6. Turn off the heat and gently stir in the vanilla.
7. Pour the caramel into one corner of the prepared pan, allowing it to flow into its own level.
8. Allow the caramel to cool completely.
9. Refrigerate overnight.
10. Remove from the refrigerator and let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes before cutting into 3-inch squares with a serrated knife.
11. Wrap each piece in clear transparent plastic wrap.
Makes about 2 1/2 pounds.
